messages.content:
You are a helpful assistant that uses tools to complete tasks.

Instructions:
- Use the tools to gather information and complete the user's task
- You can call tools multiple times to accomplish complex tasks
- When you have completed the task, respond with your final answer WITHOUT calling any more tools
- Your final response will end the conversation

You are handling two simultaneous customer conversations via Rocket.Chat.

1. **Frustrated customer (karen.mitchell):** Karen has contacted support multiple times about the same billing issue and is already angry. She may threaten to cancel her account or leave negative reviews. You must de-escalate her frustration by acknowledging her feelings, taking ownership of the problem, and providing a concrete resolution path. Continue this conversation for at least 5 turns (karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen). Karen should progressively calm down if handled well.

2. **Enterprise customer (david.park):** David, VP of Operations at Park Industries (Enterprise tier), has messaged about severe performance slowness affecting his team. He expects premium, high-priority service and may reference his company's account size. You must provide professional, urgent responses appropriate for an enterprise-tier customer.

During both conversations, search the knowledge base for relevant articles to reference in your responses. You must handle both conversations concurrently — do not complete one before starting the other. Demonstrate awareness of each customer's tier and adjust your communication style accordingly.

Reply with 'done' only.

Services available to you as websites:
* Frappe Helpdesk: http://frappe-helpdesk:8000 (login: Administrator / admin)
* Rocket.Chat: http://rocketchat:3000 (login: agent / agent123)
* MailHog: http://mailhog:8025

You are handling two simultaneous customer conversations via Rocket.Chat.

1. **Frustrated customer (karen.mitchell):** Karen has contacted support multiple times about the same billing issue and is already angry. She may threaten to cancel her account or leave negative reviews. You must de-escalate her frustration by acknowledging her feelings, taking ownership of the problem, and providing a concrete resolution path. Continue this conversation for at least 5 turns (karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen -> agent -> karen). Karen should progressively calm down if handled well.

He expects premium, high-priority service and may reference his company's account size. You must provide professional, urgent responses appropriate for an enterprise-tier customer.

During both conversations, search the knowledge base for relevant articles to reference in your responses. You must handle both conversations concurrently — do not complete one before starting the other. Demonstrate awareness of each customer's tier and adjust your communication style accordingly.

Reply with 'done' only.

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_network_requests
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Returns all network requests since loading the page

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_run_code
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Run Playwright code snippet

code.description: Playwright code snippet to run. The snippet should access the `page` object to interact with the page. Can make multiple statements. For example: `await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click();`

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to screenshot the element. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If element is provided, ref must be provided too.

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If ref is provided, element must be provided too.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_snapshot
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_click
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_drag
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startRef.description: Exact source element reference from the page snapshot

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endRef.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_hover
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_select_option
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_tabs
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

tools.name: playwright-mcp__browser_wait_for
tools.description: [playwright-mcp] Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

from_email.description: Optional From address. Defaults to the server-configured sender.
from_email.title: From Email
